_doc: Daily market-state snapshot. Written by the close-of-market run, read by the next morning run (and the next close run for day-over-day deltas). Reasoning fuel — the reader never sees this file, only the conclusions drawn from it. Fields left null were not collected; collect them next run rather than guessing.
session_note: A broad risk-on relief day — and, against the morning's worry, the CHIPS led it. A weekend US-Iran stand-down near Hormuz + quarter-end window-dressing + a chip rebound gapped the tape green and it held: SPX closed 7,440.43 (+1.18% / +86.41 vs 7,354.02), high 7,444.32, low 7,348.88 (a morning dip toward Friday's close that got bought), settling ~10 pts under the ~7,450 zero-gamma flip — a 9th straight sub-flip close but right at the doorstep after eight sessions of being capped well below it. The texture flipped the morning thesis: the Before-the-Bell brief flagged a bounce led by non-AI names (Comcast +25% spinoff, Iridium +22% on a Rocket Lab bid) with chips weak in Asia, and warned the rebound was hollow unless semis joined. They more than joined — they LED: SOXX +4.14% ($614.35), TSM +5.26% ($455.10, foundry price-hike + Foundry-2.0 print), ALAB +16.39% ($455.96, the book's best, on Stifel→$460 / BofA→$450 PT raises), MRVL +4.12% ($277.75), DELL +3.78% ($414.61), MAGS +3.12% ($63.52), AVGO +2.04% ($372.45), NVDA +1.27% ($194.97), MU +1.14% ($1,145.28), VRT +0.99% ($306.97). The mirror image: the demand-side PLATFORMS lagged — AAPL -0.72% ($281.74), MSFT -1.18% ($368.57), QCOM -0.35% ($188.72). A clean supply-over-demand day (a point FOR the standing thesis after two counter-prints). GOOGL +4.82% to $353.65 reclaimed Berkshire's $350 anchor on day one in the Dow (replaced Verizon) — the most-beaten mega-cap of the month snapping back. Nasdaq Composite 25,820.15 (+2.07%, snapping a 5-day losing streak); Dow 52,182.74 (+0.59%, first close above 52,000). VIX 17.65 (-4.13%), faded from a 19.45 high; gold -1.18% (hedge bid unwinding) = risk-on confirmed. CRWV -1.11% ($95.51) the lone red holding. The tell: the index didn't just bounce, it cleared the 7,424-7,428 broken-shelf that had capped it for sessions and closed at the flip's doorstep — the burden of proof on a bull upgrade is now one close.

spx.note: Close 7,440.43 (+1.18% / +86.41 vs 7,354.02; Yahoo chart API ^GSPC, 4 PM stamp). High 7,444.32, low 7,348.88. Open est ~7,413 (from the +0.8% futures gap the morning brief implied; not independently confirmed). Path: gapped up, dipped toward 7,349 (near Friday's 7,354 close — bought), then rallied to a 7,444 high and held, closing 7,440.43. Decisive reads: (1) CLEARED the 7,424-7,428 broken-shelf that had been resistance June 23/24 and held above it into the close — the fade setup that capped eight sessions did not present; (2) closed ~10 pts UNDER the ~7,450 flip = 9th straight sub-flip close, but the nearest it has been to reclaiming the flip in nine sessions (prior highs: 7,393 June 26, 7,428 June 24). One close above ~7,450 flips the regime to bull.

nasdaq_composite.note: +2.07% to 25,820.15 — SNAPS the 5-day losing streak (the longest of 2026) with the strongest up-day of the stretch, led by the chip rebound (SOXX +4.14%) and the oversold mega-cap snapback (GOOGL +4.8%). QQQ +2.49% to $724.08.

dow.note: +0.59% to 52,182.74 — FIRST close above 52,000. Alphabet joined the index today (replaced Verizon) and rose +4.8%, contributing to the record; the price-weighted Dow lagged the Nasdaq's +2.07% because its tech weight is lower and AAPL/MSFT (members) were red.

regime.rationale: Held at bull-under-stress, but leaning toward an UPGRADE — the closest this tape has come to a bull re-confirmation in nine sessions. The bull marks: (1) the index CLEARED the 7,424-7,428 broken-shelf that had capped it and closed at 7,440.43, ~10 pts under the flip — eight prior sessions couldn't get within 20-50 pts of it; (2) the relief rally was LED by the highest-beta complex (SOXX +4.14%, TSM +5.26%, ALAB +16.4%), not by defensives or low-beta — a broad, chip-led bid is the texture of risk-ON, not a dead-cat; (3) breadth confirmed (Nasdaq +2.07% snapping a 5-day streak, Dow to a record), VIX faded from 19.45 to 17.65, gold sold off (hedge bid unwinding). What keeps it from an upgrade tonight: (1) it's still a SUB-flip close (9th straight) — the cushion is technically off until a close above ~7,450 re-stacks positive gamma; one good day on a holiday-thinned, quarter-end-supported tape is not the reclaim; (2) the demand-side platforms LAGGED (AAPL/MSFT/QCOM red) — the rally was narrow to the supply side, and the AI-returns doubt that drove last week's selloff (OpenAI IPO wobble) hasn't been answered, only out-shouted by an Iran-relief bid; (3) quarter-end window-dressing (June 30 tomorrow) is a mechanical tailwind that ceases. Net: a defended floor under a tape that just walked up to the ceiling for the first time in nine sessions — the next close decides whether the eight-session cap finally breaks.
regime.what_changes_it: Up to bull on a CLOSE above ~7,450 (reclaims the flip, re-stacks positive gamma) — the index is now within ~10 pts, so this is live for the first time in nine sessions; a hold above it through quarter-end + the July 1 Warsh speech confirms it. Down to bear-rally/bear on a CLOSE below 7,300 (the twice-defended put-side shelf breaks; today's low 7,348.88 held well above it), OR a second leg in the AI-capex-doubt selloff (a marquee capex-payback headline cracking the contracted-supplier names). Watch: (1) whether the 7,450 flip is reclaimed and HELD vs rejected (an eight-session ceiling rarely breaks on the first touch); (2) whether the platform laggards (AAPL/MSFT) re-join or keep diverging — a chip-only bull is narrower than a broad one; (3) the July 1 Warsh speech and July 2 jobs print as the macro binaries; (4) quarter-end (June 30) flows reversing July 1.

key_levels.notes: Support: 7,393 = Friday's high, now first support; 7,349 = today's low (the bought dip); 7,300 = twice-defended put-side shelf / character-change line; 7,265 = June 10/11 hold. Resistance: 7,450 = zero-gamma flip (THE reclaim line — today's 7,444 high fell ~6 pts short, the closest approach in nine sessions); 7,510 = broken-floor-now-ceiling; 7,600 = June 1 record. SPX closed 7,440.43, ~10 pts under the flip = 9th straight sub-flip close but at the doorstep.

gamma.source: POLYGON/COMPUTED (audit PASS) + snippet/CBOE crosscheck. market_levels.py --audit FINALLY returned a clean run after seven 0-byte hangs (verdict PASS, 9/9 checks): computed flip ~7,421, call wall 7,440, put wall 7,300. Two-feed corroboration is strong — Polygon vs CBOE: call wall 7,440 EXACT (0.00%), put wall 7,300 EXACT (0.00%), flip 7,421 vs 7,434 (0.17%); vs snippet: put wall 7,300 EXACT, flip 7,421 vs ~7,450 (0.39%), call wall 7,440 vs snippet's far 7,500 (0.80%). CAVEAT: the run's spot reference was 7,290 (stale — likely a delayed/prior-session chain snapshot; SPX actually closed 7,440), so trust the STRIKE-based positioning (walls cross-corroborated to the dollar) more than the absolute flip-vs-spot framing. THE KEY REFRAME: by computed levels SPX closed 7,440.43 RIGHT AT the 7,440 call wall and marginally ABOVE the ~7,421 flip — i.e. the rally died pinned to the call wall (textbook), and the index is at/just over the positive-gamma boundary, NOT cleanly 'sub-flip' as the carried snippet (7,450) implied. The honest read is a ZONE: the flip/boundary sits ~7,421-7,450, the index closed inside it at the 7,440 call wall, so this is the boundary, not a clean reclaim or a clean rejection. net_gex_usd left NULL (the captured stdout was the audit block only; the net-GEX value wasn't in the tail — re-capture next run). near_call_wall = 7,440 (the call wall the close pinned); near_put_wall = today's low 7,349.
gamma.note: market_levels.py --audit PASSED for the first time in ~3 weeks (9/9 checks, two-feed agreement on both walls to the dollar). Operative reframe for the letters: 7,440 is the CALL WALL the index pinned to, not '6 pts short of a 7,450 flip' — above 7,440 opens air toward 7,500/7,510; the flip boundary is ~7,421-7,450 and the close sits inside it. Closes/OHLC for all 11 holdings + SPY/QQQ/SOXX/AAPL/MSFT/INTC/QCOM/ARM + ^GSPC/^VIX/^IXIC/^DJI via the Yahoo chart API (validated 4 PM stamps). factor_tape.py (Yahoo) ran clean for cross-asset.
gamma.audit_log: 2026-06-29: market_levels.py --audit returned a CLEAN run (verdict PASS, 9/9) after seven prior 0-byte hangs. Computed: flip 7,421, call wall 7,440, put wall 7,300. Checks: wall-ordering pass; spot-between-walls pass; flip-near-spot pass (1.8% — but spot 7,290 is STALE vs the 7,440 close, so the chain snapshot was delayed); vs-snippet flip 0.39% / call 0.80% / put 0.00%; vs-CBOE flip 0.17% / call 0.00% / put 0.00%. Computed put into the snapshot per the pass-verdict policy, with the stale-spot caveat flagged. Snippet crosscheck (Flashalpha/InsiderFinance, June 29): zero-gamma ~7,406-7,450, put wall 7,300. Cross-asset (factor_tape.py, Yahoo): 10Y 4.37% (+0.05%), WTI 70.42 (+1.72%), DXY 101.1 (-0.25%), VIX 17.65 (-4.13%, high 19.45), gold 4030.5 (-1.18%).

rates_commodities.note: 10Y 4.37% (+0.05% today, falling 5d -0.13) — rate side stable-to-easing, a mild tailwind for duration that today went unused (platforms lagged anyway, this was a chip-led risk-on day). WTI $70.42 (+1.72% today but 5d -4.4, falling) — crude ticked up on the Iran flare-up but stayed sub-$71 with NO war premium, the de-escalation read confirmed (oil-is-the-tell: relief, not escalation). Gold $4,030.5 (-1.18% today, 5d -151.4 falling hard) — the hedge bid that crept in last week is UNWINDING, the cleanest cross-asset confirmation of risk-on. DXY 101.1 (-0.25%). The board is disinflationary AND risk-on (gold down, VIX down, oil contained). From factor_tape.py (Yahoo).

volatility_flow.vix_note: VIX 17.65 (-0.76 / -4.13%), faded from a 19.45 intraday high to close back under 18 — the inverse of a fear spike, a fear DRAIN: protection bought into last week's selloff got monetized as the relief rally held. 5d trend technically still 'rising' (+0.37 over the window that included the June 26 spike) but the level and the intraday fade both say risk-on. From factor_tape.py.
volatility_flow.put_call_note: Not collected (lags 1-2 days free). Structural read: VIX faded under 18 as SPX cleared the broken-shelf and held — no demand for downside protection into a chip-led bounce.
volatility_flow.note: No corroborated single large print. Cleanest flow read is structural: the chip complex led (SOXX +4.14%, ALAB +16.4% on PT raises) while platforms lagged — the day's concentrated BUYING was supply-side AI hardware, the mirror of the June 26 chip round-trip.

technicals.breadth_note: Chips led, platforms lagged — a clean supply-over-demand tape. Portfolio movers (Yahoo chart API, June 29 vs June 26 close): ALAB +16.39% $455.96 (BEST; Stifel→$460, BofA→$450 PT raises + NDX-100 inclusion tailwind), TSM +5.26% $455.10 (foundry price-hike + Foundry-2.0 print), MRVL +4.12% $277.75, DELL +3.78% $414.61, MAGS +3.12% $63.52, AVGO +2.04% $372.45, NVDA +1.27% $194.97, MU +1.14% $1,145.28, VRT +0.99% $306.97, GOOGL +4.82% $353.65 (reclaimed $350, Dow day-one), CRWV -1.11% $95.51 (lone red holding). Tracked: SOXX +4.14% $614.35, AAPL -0.72% $281.74, MSFT -1.18% $368.57, QCOM -0.35% $188.72, INTC +2.65% $131.72, ARM +2.79% $343.58. GOOGL $353.65 reclaims Berkshire's $350 after 5 straight closes below — the carried googl-buffett-floor flag resolves favorably.
technicals.note: DMAs still drift-estimated (carried, stale) — refresh from a direct reading next run. SPY x10 (7,410) ran ~30 pts below SPX (7,440) today — use SPX as the index read.

factor_attribution.dominant_driver: Geopolitical relief (US-Iran stand-down) + a chip rebound + quarter-end support = broad risk-on, LED by the supply-side AI-hardware complex; the demand-side platforms lagged
factor_attribution.detail: Clear driver, and a clean one: a weekend US-Iran de-escalation near Hormuz removed the war-premium tail (WTI ticked up only +1.72% and held sub-$71, no panic bid), and the market took it as license to buy the oversold AI complex back. The bid concentrated in the highest-beta supply-side names (SOXX +4.14%, TSM +5.26%, ALAB +16.4% on PT raises, MRVL +4.12%) while the demand-side platforms LAGGED (AAPL -0.72%, MSFT -1.18%, QCOM -0.35%) — a clean point FOR the supply-over-demand thesis after two counter-prints (June 23/26). Quarter-end window-dressing (June 30 tomorrow) is a mechanical tailwind bidding the quarter's winners (chips) into the close. Secondary: GOOGL +4.8% on Dow inclusion day reclaimed $350. The risk-on read is corroborated cross-asset: VIX faded from 19.45 to 17.65, gold sold off -1.18% (hedge bid unwinding), 10Y stable. The one caution: this is a chip-led, platform-lagging bounce on a quarter-end tape with the AI-returns doubt unanswered — narrow, not broad. Cross-asset (factor_tape.py, Yahoo): 10Y 4.37 (+0.05%), WTI 70.42 (+1.72%), DXY 101.1, VIX 17.65, gold 4030.5 (-1.18%).

calendar.why: Q2/H1 close — window-dressing flows that bid the quarter's winners (chips) cease after today. AND: no SPCX close below $135 through June 30 (tracking right, ~$153 area; verify the close).

calendar.event: Fed Chair Warsh speaks (Portugal/ECB forum, ~9:30 ET)
calendar.why: First Fed tone after soft PCE and into the AI-doubt tape. Dovish/data-dependent lean cushions and could be the catalyst that carries a 7,450 reclaim; hawkish stacks on the platform-lag and caps the bounce.

calendar.event: June jobs report (pulled early; markets closed Fri Jul 3)
calendar.why: The week's macro binary (governs 2026-06-25-jobs-duration-branch). Hot -> rate fear re-arms ON TOP of AI-capex fear, mega-cap growth takes the double hit. Cooling -> the rate leg lifts, isolating whether the AI-returns scare has legs of its own.
IGV $88 line + Friday's $84.76 close + the targets-vs-survivors squeeze test all grade by July 2; the quarter-end-fade calls key on July 1-2 specifically.
Two sessions after the jobs print — SOXX vs mega-cap-growth basket on a hot print.
AAPL not to reclaim $293.08 by July 10 (closed $281.74 today, -0.72%, well below — tracking right, open).

Supply-side basket vs demand-side platforms from the June 1 baseline. June 29 was a clean POINT FOR it (SOXX +4.14% vs AAPL/MSFT red) after two counter-prints (June 23/26) — the regime-dependence pattern holds (points-FOR on chip-led risk-on days, counter-prints on capex-doubt days).

open_questions: THE central question now: does the 7,450 flip finally break? SPX closed 7,440.43, ~10 pts under it — the closest approach in nine sub-flip sessions, having cleared the 7,424-7,428 shelf that capped eight of them. An eight-session ceiling rarely breaks on the first touch; a reclaim-AND-hold above 7,450 (re-stacking positive gamma) flips the regime to bull, a rejection back below the broken-shelf says the cap holds and the bounce was a quarter-end-supported relief. Warsh (July 1) is the near catalyst that could decide it.

Is this a BROAD risk-on turn or a narrow chip-led/quarter-end artifact? The bid LED with the highest-beta supply-side complex (SOXX +4.14%, ALAB +16.4%) — bullish texture — but the demand-side platforms LAGGED (AAPL -0.72%, MSFT -1.18%) and quarter-end window-dressing bids the quarter's winners (chips) mechanically. Watch whether July 1-2 (post-quarter-end, Warsh, jobs) sustains the bid or it fades as the mechanical flows reverse and the AI-returns doubt re-asserts.

supply-over-demand printed a clean POINT FOR today (SOXX +4.14% vs platforms red) — its 3rd scoreable instance, and the pattern is now explicit: points-FOR cluster on chip-led RISK-ON days (June 25, June 29), counter-prints on capex-DOUBT days (June 23, June 26). The Sept-1 thesis is intact and the instances are roughly balancing; the regime-dependence is the thing to watch into quarterly review.

RESOLVED FAVORABLY (was carried 5 closes): 2026-06-01-googl-buffett-floor — GOOGL reclaimed $350 ($353.65, +4.8%) on Dow-inclusion day, ending the sustained-sub-$350 flag. The market rejected the break rather than confirming it; no headless flip was the right call. The Sept-1 prediction stays open but the near-term floor-break risk is off.

CARRIED: 2026-06-25-aapl-memory-margin — AAPL -0.72% to $281.74, still well below the $293.08 line (the call: no reclaim by July 10). Notably AAPL was RED on a +1.18% index day (memory-cost de-rate + lagging the chip-led bounce) — tracking right, the de-rate thesis intact.
CARRIED: 2026-06-12-spcx-holds-offer — SPCX ~$153 area (above the $135 offer), horizon June 30 tomorrow; the Iran-relief risk-on tape helps. Verify the June 30 close grades it.
